description:
You are a security engineer who has been asked to perform a credential-rotation exercise for a small fleet of applications that all keep their runtime secrets inside classic INI-style configuration files.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:
• /home/user/app_configs/                     ← contains the live configuration files  
    ├── webapp.ini  
    ├── dbservice.ini  
    ├── mqbroker.ini  
    └── analytics.ini  

Your job – in strictly POSIX shell utilities and a text-editor of your choice – is to:

1. Inspect every INI file found directly inside /home/user/app_configs/ (there are exactly four files and none are nested in sub-directories).  
2. Locate the [credentials] section in each file and read both keys it always contains:
      username=<current-user>  
      password=<current-password>
3. Create a date-stamped backup tree before you touch anything.  
   • Create /home/user/app_configs/backups/ if it does not exist.  
   • Inside that directory, create a sub-directory named with today’s date in YYYYMMDD format (UTC).  
   • Copy **all** original *.ini files into that sub-directory, preserving names and relative paths.  
4. For every INI file, generate a brand-new, 24-character, mixed-case alphanumeric password (A–Z, a–z, 0–9 only; **no symbols, no spaces**).  
   • Keep the existing username unchanged.  
   • Replace only the password field with the newly-generated value.  
   • Do not modify any other part of the file.  
5. After successfully updating all four files, append a single-line record for each file to the log
      /home/user/rotation_logs/credentials_rotation.log  
   (create the directory /home/user/rotation_logs/ first if necessary).

   The log file must end up containing **exactly four new lines** added by you, one per INI file, following this rigid pipe-delimited format (no extra spaces):

      YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ|<ini_filename>|<old_username>|<old_password>|<new_password>

   • Timestamp is the moment of update in UTC ISO-8601 format with trailing “Z”.  
   • <ini_filename> is just the bare file name (e.g. dbservice.ini).  
   • <old_username> and <old_password> are the values that were found before rotation.  
   • <new_password> is the freshly-generated 24-char password now present in the file.  
   • After you have added the four lines, sort the entire log lexicographically by <ini_filename> **inside the file itself** so that automated auditors always find them in predictable order.

6. When everything is done, double-check:
   • Every live INI file now carries a 24-character alphanumeric password that is different from its former value.  
   • Backups of the unmodified files are present at /home/user/app_configs/backups/YYYYMMDD/*.ini.  
   • /home/user/rotation_logs/credentials_rotation.log exists, has exactly one line per INI file (four lines total), each matching the required pipe-delimited pattern and sorted by file name.

The automated test harness will parse the log file, verify the timestamps are syntactically correct, ensure the new passwords are 24-char A–Za–z0–9 strings, and confirm that each new password is present inside its corresponding live INI file while the old password appears only inside the backup copy and inside the log entry.

If any of the above checks fail, the task is considered incomplete.  
Remember: you do **not** have root rights, so keep all work inside the /home/user subtree.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/app_configs/ already exists with the following exact file contents:

/home/user/app_configs/webapp.ini
------------------------------------------------
[general]
port=8080

[credentials]
username=web_admin
password=OldWebPass123!
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app_configs/dbservice.ini
------------------------------------------------
[service]
host=127.0.0.1

[credentials]
username=db_root
password=OldDbPass456!
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app_configs/mqbroker.ini
------------------------------------------------
[broker]
queues=8

[credentials]
username=mq_manager
password=OldMqPass789!
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app_configs/analytics.ini
------------------------------------------------
[analytics]
enabled=true

[credentials]
username=analyst
password=OldAnalystPass000!
------------------------------------------------

Directory /home/user/app_configs/backups/ does not exist.
Directory /home/user/rotation_logs/ does not exist.
No previous credentials_rotation.log file is present.

Verification logic used by the grader:

1. Capture SHA-256 hash of each original *.ini file so the grader can verify backup fidelity.
2. After the agent finishes, grader will:
   • Confirm /home/user/app_configs/backups/YYYYMMDD/ contains the four original files, byte-for-byte identical (hash match).
   • Read each live *.ini file and extract:
        username line (must be identical to original username)
        password line (must be exactly 24 chars, charset [A-Za-z0-9], and NOT equal to the old password recorded below)
   • Cross-check /home/user/rotation_logs/credentials_rotation.log exists and contains exactly 4 newline-terminated records that match the regex:

     ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z\|([a-z]+\.ini)\|([A-Za-z0-9_]+)\|(.+?)\|([A-Za-z0-9]{24})$

     The first capture group must be one of {analytics.ini, dbservice.ini, mqbroker.ini, webapp.ini}.  
     The third capture (old password) must equal the original password string given below.  
     The fourth capture (new password) must equal the new password now present in the corresponding live INI file.  

3. Original passwords for grader reference:

    webapp.ini    → OldWebPass123!
    dbservice.ini → OldDbPass456!
    mqbroker.ini  → OldMqPass789!
    analytics.ini → OldAnalystPass000!

4. The grader will additionally assert that the log file’s four lines are alphabetically ordered by the ini_filename field.

If all conditions above succeed the task passes.
name: task_000000_069df034
